title: GAVEL: Grounded Caption Error Verification and Localization
abstract: arXiv:2606.26923v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Vision-language models (VLMs) often produce hallucinated or inconsistent outputs, where text and images are not properly aligned. Addressing this issue requires not only detecting misalignment but also explaining the discrepancy and localizing its visual evidence. We introduce GAVEL (Grounded Caption Error Verification and Localization), a task that jointly addresses verification, explanation, and localization for image-text pairs. To support systematic evaluation, we also present a corresponding dataset and benchmark. We further train a supervised baseline on the human-annotated training split to assess whether GAVEL provides learnable supervision for these abilities. Experiments show that even strong closed-source models struggle on GAVEL, while the supervised baseline yields consistent improvements across grounding and explanation metrics.

Title: GAVEL: Grounded Caption Error Verification and Localization
Abstract: arXiv:2606.26923v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Vision-language models (VLMs) often produce hallucinated or inconsistent outputs, where text and images are not properly aligned. Addressing this issue requires not only detecting misalignment but also explaining the discrepancy and localizing its visual evidence. We introduce GAVEL (Grounded Caption Error Verification and Localization), a task that jointly addresses verification, explanation, and localization for image-text pairs. To support systematic evaluation, we also present a corresponding dataset and benchmark. We further train a supervised baseline on the human-annotated training split to assess whether GAVEL provides learnable supervision for these abilities. Experiments show that even strong closed-source models struggle on GAVEL, while the supervised baseline yields consistent improvements across grounding and explanation metrics.
translated_title: GAVEL：基于视觉定位的字幕错误验证与定位
translated_abstract: 视觉语言模型（VLM）常产生幻觉或不一致的输出，导致文本与图像未能正确对齐。解决此问题不仅需要检测错位，还需解释差异并定位其视觉证据。我们提出了GAVEL（基于视觉定位的字幕错误验证与定位）任务，该任务联合处理图像-文本对的验证、解释和定位。为支持系统评估，我们还提供了相应的数据集和基准。我们进一步在人工标注的训练集上训练了一个有监督基线模型，以评估GAVEL是否为这些能力提供了可学习的监督信号。实验表明，即使强大的闭源模型在GAVEL任务上也表现困难，而监督基线在定位和解释指标上均取得了一致的改进。
tldr: 提出GAVEL任务，联合处理图像-文本对的错误验证、解释和视觉定位，并构建数据集和基准，实验表明监督基线优于强闭源模型。
en_tdlr: GAVEL proposes a joint task of verification, explanation, and visual localization for image-text misalignment, with a dataset and benchmark showing that supervised baselines outperform strong closed-source models.
